notes: Three vibe lifetimes back-to-back — variant 1 (clean exit via Ctrl+D/`/exit`) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle) → variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn). CORRECTION: vibe is a PRESESSION adapter (not single-birth as previously assumed) — each variant is born as a proc-<PID> pre-session in ready (vN_session_birth), then the real session_<ts>_<hash> dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id (vN_handoff new_session, asserted never to enter waiting over its lifetime), then the real session drives ready→working (vN_turn_start). Each variant's process_exited anchors the next variant's birth; a turn_done anchors to its turn_start so the matcher finds the post-turn ready, not the handoff ready. Session-end signal is process_exited (transcripts persist on disk). Variant chain templated from the codex sibling. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Session end (3 variants); mirrors the codex sibling's variant chain

text: Variant 1: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Variant 1: real session_<ts>_<hash> dir reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting through end of its lifetime
Variant 1: first user prompt drives the real session ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean exit (Ctrl-D / /exit) — process_exited fires on the real session AFTER state returned to ready (the v1_turn_done → v1_exit chain proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: real session dir reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting over its lifetime
Variant 2: user prompt drives the real session ready → working

Variant 2: turn completes back to ready; over the ensuing idle window (through exit) the session never re-enters working or waiting (satisfies 'state never entered working post-turn before the SIGKILL')
Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires on the real session while it is in ready
Variant 3: new proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: real session dir reconciles (different session_id); never enters waiting over its lifetime
Variant 3: user prompt drives the real session ready → working
Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — process_exited fires on the real session while it is still in working (live sweep-to-ready may not appear in the recording's tail)
